original_hypothesis.title: Spot Price TVL Manipulation via Malicious Manager
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Multiple connectors (Balancer, Aerodrome, Curve) calculate TVL using spot balances or spot prices. While the lack of a public mempool prevents standard front-running, a malicious manager can manipulate the AMM pool, execute the TVL calculation, and restore the pool in a single transaction.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This highlights the extreme trust placed in the manager role. Because the manager controls the timing of `calculateWithdrawShares`, they can atomically manipulate the oracle, calculate their own withdrawal at an inflated price, and steal funds.
preconditions: Manager is malicious or manager keys are compromised
Vault has positions in AMMs using spot pricing

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: TVL relies on easily manipulable spot balances
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Manager uses flash loan to imbalance pool, calls `calculateWithdrawShares`, repays loan
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Manager withdraws massive excess of base tokens

economic_questions: Which pools are most susceptible to spot manipulation?
Can the Watcher role detect and revert this atomically?
validation_requirements: Simulate a manager-executed flash loan manipulation and withdrawal calculation

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Majority of the vault's base tokens

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires deep flash loan liquidity for the target AMM tokens
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When vault has high base token reserves

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All base tokens in the vault

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
